growth_records.strain: Enrichment from municipal thermophilic anaerobic digester (Vancouver, Canada), reactors R1/R2, day 200-300
growth_records.medium: Plugge (2005) anoxic bicarbonate-buffered basal medium + sodium acetate; TAN adjusted to 1.0 g NH4+-N/L

up.method: calculated: qS_vol = D*(Sin-Sout) = (1/30 day^-1)*(75-2.0 mM) = 2.433 mmol/L/day = 0.1014 mmol/L/h

sec.method: calculated from Table 1 methane production rate 38 ml/L/day STP: 38/22.4 = 1.696 mmol/L/day = 0.0707 mmol/L/h

yields.note: chemostat biomass yield not reported directly; see batch microcosm record for community yield 0.01 g-VSS/g-acetate

growth_records.cite: McDaniel et al. Diverse electron carriers drive syntrophic interactions in an enriched anaerobic acetate-oxidizing consortium. The ISME Journal (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41396-023-01542-6. [PMC10689502]
growth_records.snippet: chemostat reactors held at 55 °C and fed with anaerobic medium containing acetate... Low acetate levels in the effluent (~2 mM) relative to the feed (75 mM)... Table 1: Influent acetate 75 mM; HRT 30 days; pH 8.0+/-0.3; Methane production r
growth_records.notes: Chemostat consortium (mixed community), values are averages of duplicate reactors at steady state (days 200-300). D=1/HRT=1/30 day^-1=0.03333 day^-1=0.001389 h^-1; at chemostat steady state with SRT=HRT, community mu=D=0.001389 h^-1. td=ln2/mu=0.6931/0.001389=499 h (=20.8 days). Acetate consumed = influent 75 mM - effluent 2.0 mM = 73 mM; volumetric uptake qS=D*(Sin-Sout)=0.03333*73=2.433 mmol/L/day=0.1014 mmol/L/h. Methane from Table 1 = 38 ml/L/day STP; /22.4 L/mol = 1.696 mmol/L/day = 0.0707 mmol/L/h. Per-gDW specific rates not derivable (only total solids 8.5 g/L reported, not biomass VSS). Full Plugge basal medium recipe is in Supplementary Methods (not in main text), so medium_composition_stated=false.

growth_records.medium: Anoxic sterilized basal medium + 50 mM acetate (SIP microcosm)

up.method: calculated: acetate fell 3000->280 mg/L over 408 h; (3000-280)/60 g/mol = 45.33 mM / 408 h = 0.111 mmol/L/h

sec.method: calculated: 17.5 mL CH4/microcosm over 408 h; 17.5/22400 = 0.781 mmol per 0.020 L = 39.06 mmol/L; /408 h = 0.0957 mmol/L/h

growth_records.cite: McDaniel et al. Diverse electron carriers drive syntrophic interactions in an enriched anaerobic acetate-oxidizing consortium. The ISME Journal (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41396-023-01542-6. [PMC10689502]
growth_records.snippet: Stable isotope probing with 13C-labeled acetate (50 mM; 3000 mg/l)... Over the 408-h incubation, the acetate-fed microcosms produced an average of 17.5 ml CH4 (at STP)... acetate concentrations decreased from 3000 mg/l to 280 +/- 136 mg/l o
growth_records.notes: Batch SIP microcosm, mixed 3-member consortium. Acetate 50 mM = 3000 mg/L (implies acetate MW=60). Consumed = 3000-280 = 2720 mg/L over 408 h = 2720/60 = 45.33 mM; volumetric qS = 45.33/408 = 0.111 mmol/L/h. CH4 = 17.5 mL/microcosm STP over 408 h; liquid volume ~20 mL; 17.5 mL/22400 mL/mol = 0.7813 mmol per microcosm; /0.020 L = 39.06 mmol/L; /408 h = 0.0957 mmol/L/h. Reported community biomass yield Yxs = 0.01 g-VSS/g-acetate (=0.015 g-COD/g-COD, assuming protein/biomass 0.5 and VSS/COD 1.42). Theoretical acetate->methane conversion 88%. Community pFBA model gives 0.706 mol ATP/mol acetate and predicts DTU068_1 consumes 1 mol acetate -> 1 mol formate intracellularly (0.824 mol oxidized via FdhA-NuoEF, 0.045 mol formate excreted via FdhA-FdxH-CytB) plus H2 + CO2; these are model predictions, not measured fluxes. Per-organism specific rates (mmol/gDW/h) not measured. Growth rate not directly measured; doubling time not derivable from batch data reported.

carbon_growth.medium: Plugge (2005) anoxic bicarbonate-buffered basal medium + sodium acetate; TAN adj
carbon_growth.citation: McDaniel et al. Diverse electron carriers drive syntrophic interactions in an enriched anaerobic acetate-oxidizing consortium. The ISME Journal (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41396-023-01542
